Category: Manufacturing: Chemicals, Food Manufacturing, Industrial Manufacturing, Textiles, Automotive, Pharmaceuticals, Electronics
Who is this for: Supply Chain Managers, Procurement Specialists, Logistics Coordinators, Operations Managers, Manufacturing Engineers, Plant Managers, Production Planners, Inventory Control Analysts
Team or Dept this is for: Operations, Supply Chain, Procurement, Manufacturing, Logistics, Production Planning
Description: Risks in forecasting, procurement, and logistics automation.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with manufacturing or supply chain operations, along with comfort using everyday digital tools such as spreadsheets or inventory software, is all you need to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key risks associated with AI-driven forecasting, procurement, and logistics automation across manufacturing sectors such as chemicals, food production, automotive, and pharmaceuticals. You will also be able to explain how AI technologies are applied within supply chains and apply practical strategies to manage and mitigate those risks in real-world manufacturing environments.
